sales.title: SASANIAN KINGDOM. Shahpur (Sabuhr) II (AD 309-379). AV dinar (20mm, 7.22 gm, 2h). NGC AU 3/5 - 4/5.  Uncertain mint, ca. AD 320. Bust of Shahpur II right, seen from front, wearing mural crown with korymbos / Fire altar tied with ribbons; flanked by incense burners. Sunrise 863. Göbl Ia/7.  Shahpur II (alternatively Sabuhr) reigned from 309 AD to 379 AD, the longest tenure in Iranian history, initiating a Golden Age of prosperity and military success for his empire. After his campaigns against the Arab tribes in 325 AD, Shahpur II ordered the construction of a line of fortifications across southern Mesopotamia, called the Wall of the Arabs. Thus, the western coastline of the Persian Gulf was established as the new province of Bahrayn. Thereafter Shahpur II was preoccupied for several decades in wars at the western frontiers with the Roman Emperor Constantius II. His successor, Julian the Apostate, advanced on to Ctesiphon with a huge host, but was summarily defeated and killed in battle by the Sasanians; a military victory immortalized in a rock relief at Bishapur. The subsequent peace treaty with Jovian in 363 AD ceded control of Roman Armenia and Georgia (Iberia) to the Sasanian Empire. During this time the Sasanians had also warred with the Kidarite Huns, pushing eastward past Bactria into the Hindu Kush. The territorial extent of the Sassanian Empire reached its zenith under Shahpur II.   HID05401242017  © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

SASANIAN KINGDOM. Shahpur (Sabuhr) II (AD 309-379). AV dinar (19mm, 7.13 gm, 2h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 3/5, brushed. Uncertain mint, ca. AD 320. Bust of Shahpur II right, seen from front, wearing mural crown with korymbos / Fire altar tied with ribbons; flanked by incense burners. Sunrise 863. Göbl Ia/7.  Shahpur II (alternatively Sabuhr) reigned from 309 AD to 379 AD, the longest tenure in Iranian history, initiating a Golden Age of prosperity and military success for his empire. During this time the Sasanians had also warred with the Kidarite Huns, pushing eastward past Bactria into the Hindu Kush.
